chemistry.reaction_name: Oxidative C,H-Bond Activation of N-aryl tetrahydroisoquinoline
chemistry.scope_summary: Flow photoredox catalysis demonstrated for oxidative C,H-bond activations, intra- and intermolecular radical reactions, and atom transfer radical additions with improved reaction times and reproducibility compared to batch processes

figures.key_information: Two-step reaction sequence: (1) photocatalytic bromination using Ru(bpy)3Cl2 catalyst (0.5 mol%), BrCCl3 (3 equiv), blue LED light in DMF to form iminium bromide salt (compound 3), followed by (2) nucleophilic substitution with MeNO2 in 89% yield to form nitromethyl product (compound 4)
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Oxidation of tetrahydroisoquinoline in flow followed by aza- Henry reaction. [7]
Figure 1.  Oxidation of tetrahydroisoquinoline in flow followed by aza- Henry reaction. [7]

Figure 2.  Flow photochemical deoxygenation of nucleosides to 2 ′ ,3 ′ -dideoxynucleosides in a medium pressure Hg lamp and cus- tomized coils of quartz tubing. [9] | Figure 3.  Accelerated photoreduction in a flow reactor. [11]

figures.key_information: Photochemical transformation from compound 22 to compound 23 with 80% isolated yield using 400 W medium pressure Hg lamp with Vycor filter in MeCN at 8 mL/min flow rate
figures.caption: Figure 7.  Intermolecular [2 + 2] photocycloaddition in UV-transpar- ent FEP tubing in a Hg lamp immersion reactor. [17] | Figure 8.  Intramolecular [5 + 2] photocyloaddition in multi-layered FEP tubing wrapped around a 400 W medium pressure Hg vapor discharge lamp. [17] | Figure 9.  Intramolecular [2 + 5] photocycloaddition as a key step in the total synthesis of a  Stemona  alkaloid. [18]   The insert shows a typical multi-layered FEP tubing photoreactor setup.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: This is a review paper covering multiple photochemical reactions rather than a single batch-to-flow translation
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Better light penetration, improved temperature control, shorter reaction times, easier scale-up, and the ability to remove products in-line to avoid secondary decompositions
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction format from batch to continuous flow with improved process control and efficiency
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Core photochemical reaction mechanisms and substrate scope
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous processing with smaller reaction volumes and better temperature control

figure_descriptions: Multiple reactor schemes showing various flow photochemistry setups including PFA tubing, FEP tubing, quartz reactors, and LED/Hg lamp configurations
Mechanistic schemes for photoredox and photochemical transformations
Two-step reaction sequences with flow processing

conflicts: This is a review paper covering multiple different reactions rather than a single batch-to-flow comparison case study
notes: This is a review paper covering multiple photochemical transformations in flow rather than a single reaction optimization study. The data extracted represents one example reaction (tetrahydroisoquinoline oxidation) but the paper contains many other examples with different conditions and results.
